A.name: Model Collapse Theory
A.description: Recursive training degradation, variance growth, tail collapse, MAD, self-consuming models. Serves §2.1

B.name: Web Data Pollution & Scale
B.description: AI-generated content fraction on web, CommonCrawl contamination estimates, bot traffic, synthetic text prevalence. Serves §2.2

C.name: Detection & Reactive Limits
C.description: AI text detection, watermarking, detector limitations — used ONLY to argue reactive solutions are insufficient. Compressed role. Serves §2.2 (one paragraph)

D.name: Information Theory + Text Quality Metrics
D.description: n-gram entropy, perplexity, KL divergence, Rényi divergence, TTR, text diversity measurement tools. Serves §3 (L_auth framework) and §4 (methodology)

E.name: Data Quality & Curation for Training
E.description: Data curation, deduplication, quality filtering, data mixing ratios, scaling laws, Phi textbook-quality approach. Serves §2.3 + §5 background

F.name: Human Data Value & RLHF
F.description: RLHF cost (3.1x compute), preference data quality, human annotation vs synthetic labeling, human-in-the-loop, alignment tax. Serves §5 motivation

G.name: Platform & Provenance Design
G.description: Data provenance verification, community platforms, anti-algorithmic design, append-only data systems. Compressed — only §6 background. Serves §6

H.name: Temporal Web Quality Measurement
H.description: NEW — Corpus linguistics temporal analysis, CommonCrawl quality over time, web text entropy trends, longitudinal corpus studies. Directly serves §4 (Beat 2) experimental design.

I.name: Social Reasoning Benchmarks
I.description: NEW — SocialIQA, EmpatheticDialogues, social intelligence evaluation, social reasoning tasks, empathy benchmarks. Directly serves §5 (Beat 4) experiment design.

J.name: Fine-tune Data Composition Ablation
J.description: NEW — LoRA/QLoRA data composition studies, training set quality vs quantity ablations, Phi textbook-quality pretraining, data mixture effects on downstream tasks. Directly serves §5 (Beat 4) methodology.

_note: 10 categories, ~210 total. Every paper has a clear section assignment. H/I/J are NEW categories that were completely missing from v1.
